mnemonics: Backward Brainstorming: Start from the end and reason backward to find the perfect path.
keyPoints: Sub-game perfection requires that strategies form a Nash Equilibrium in every sub-game, not just the overall game.
The standard solution technique is Backward Induction, analyzing the game from the final decision nodes back to the beginning.
These equilibria are found in finite games of perfect information, meaning players observe all previous actions.
commonMistakes: Applying backward induction to games of imperfect information, where it is not a valid solution concept.
Assuming a strategy is sub-game perfect just because it is a Nash Equilibrium of the whole game.
Forgetting to check every sub-game, even those that would not be reached by the equilibrium path of play.
